title: cynoglossum spelaeum
intro: Cynoglossum spelaeum, also known as cave lipwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family, characterized by its small, blue to purple flowers and a distinctive leaf shape. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of Cynoglossum spelaeum are thought to include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are believed to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various ailments.
